talk_metadata.talk_title: Accelerating Molecular Glue Degrader Discovery with Cryo‑EM, Mass Spectrometry, and Computational Modeling
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we're now on less than $500 per molecule, and probably less than that"
Speaker said: "the compounds still have some PK challenges, so we didn't quite fully cover the target"
Speaker said: "we focused this talk really on cereblon, but we have similar data on other ligases. Not as much, but we're building that"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: PK properties limiting in vivo exposure were not fully optimized
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "These compounds still have some PK challenges, so we didn't quite fully cover the target"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Reliance on cereblon limits applicability to targets that can be recruited by this E3 ligase
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing primarily cereblon degraders without a parallel strategy for other E3 ligases may dilute translational impact

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data for the KAT2A degrader beyond a single PDX experiment
Limited data on off‑target degradation beyond the presented proteomics panels

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for biomarkers to predict which tumors are dependent on KAT2A versus KAT2B, enabling patient selection for selective degraders
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "in paralog screening data ... most of the activity really comes from the A component"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate proteomic dependency data with structural predictions to define a KAT2A‑dependency signature

crispro_opportunity.description: PK challenges of the KAT2A degrader could be addressed by redesign using CrisPRO‑guided scaffold optimization
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "These compounds still have some PK challenges, so we didn't quite fully cover the target"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO predicts how small‑molecule modifications affect degrader‑induced ternary complex stability and pharmacokinetic properties

crispro_opportunity.description: Extending the degradomics pipeline to non‑cereblon E3 ligases where CrisPRO can model novel ternary interfaces
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we have similar data on other ligases. Not as much, but we're building that"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s computational spine can generate predictive models for any E3‑ligase substrate receptor given structural inputs

cited_competitors.name: Neomorph
cited_competitors.context: Company founded by speaker focusing on molecular glue degraders

cited_competitors.name: Triana
cited_competitors.context: Other companies presenting similar glue strategies at the conference

cited_competitors.name: PROTAC platforms
cited_competitors.context: Compared to molecular glues for selectivity challenges

unresolved_questions: How can the degradomics pipeline be efficiently expanded to E3 ligases beyond cereblon?
What structural features determine selectivity between KAT2A and KAT2B beyond the single tyrosine interaction?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanistic Insights into PI3‑Kinase Activation by RTKs, RAS, and Membrane Context
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we were stuck at relatively low resolution, so we couldn't really resolve anything"
Speaker said: "the active site of PI3‑kinase actually was quite far away from the membrane surface, and that worried us a little bit"
Speaker said: "we don't fully understand why some patients escaped"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of structural knowledge of the fully active, membrane‑engaged conformation limited rational inhibitor design

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: These molecules target the conserved kinase pocket and therefore suffer from limited selectivity and resistance mutations
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad‑label trials without biomarker‑selected cohorts may dilute signal, especially given mutation‑specific activation mechanisms

key_data_gaps: No functional cellular validation of the dimeric active PI3‑kinase state
Limited data on how different oncogenic mutations alter the membrane‑engaged activation trajectory

crispro_opportunity.description: HR1047R mutation drives helix‑12 swinging; CrisPRO can model how this mutation stabilizes the active conformation for targeted inhibitor design
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the histidine 1047 mutation ... allows swinging of the helix and adopting this active conformation"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predictive modeling of mutant‑specific conformational ensembles to design molecules that re‑stabilize the inactive state

crispro_opportunity.description: Differential dependence on RTK versus RAS recruitment suggests a need for mutation‑specific biomarkers
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the helical domain mutations retain pretty high dependency on phosphorylated RTKs"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate structural activation states with phosphoproteomic signatures to define patient subsets

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate membrane‑mimetic nanodisc assays into early‑phase trials to select compounds that achieve full membrane‑engaged activation blockade
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we used lipid nanodiscs to reconstitute the active complex"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate compound effects on membrane‑bound conformations to prioritize candidates before clinical testing

cited_competitors.name: Alpelisib
cited_competitors.context: Existing PI3‑kinase inhibitor with limited efficacy

cited_competitors.name: Genentech PI3K programs
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned during Q&A about mutation activation

unresolved_questions: What is the physiological relevance of the PI3‑kinase dimer observed at the edge of the nanodisc?
How do different membrane curvatures influence PI3‑kinase activation and dimerization?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Conformational Landscapes of Protein Kinases Revealed by High‑Field NMR and Therapeutic Exploitation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have a multitude of different conformational states, and most of them will be populated only at a very small percentage"
Speaker said: "Gleevec binds very selectively to this low populated state"
Speaker said: "we can now design selective inhibitors for this state"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Inability to exploit low‑populated inactive conformations that confer selectivity, leading to off‑target toxicity
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker implied that many inhibitors suffer from poor selectivity due to targeting the conserved active conformation

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: High‑field NMR platform for detecting invisible states
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Requires specialized 1.1 GHz spectrometers and expertise, limiting scalability and rapid throughput
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on structural discovery without integrated functional validation may delay translation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that low‑populated inactive states exist and are druggable, then concluded that most kinases remain “undruggable” by conventional approaches

key_data_gaps: Lack of cellular validation that targeting the identified invisible states yields therapeutic benefit
No data on pharmacokinetics or toxicity of compounds designed against these states

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can computationally predict low‑populated kinase conformations, complementing NMR and accelerating hit identification
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we can now design selective inhibitors for this state"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s conformational ensemble modeling can generate virtual libraries targeting the same invisible pockets identified by NMR

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply the invisible‑state targeting approach to kinases implicated in resistant cancers where current inhibitors fail
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Gleevec binds very selectively to this low populated state"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to map invisible states across the kinome and match them to disease‑specific mutation profiles

cited_competitors.name: Gleevec (Imatinib)
cited_competitors.context: Cited as a successful example of targeting a low‑populated inactive state

unresolved_questions: What is the prevalence of druggable low‑populated states across the entire human kinome?
Can the identified invisible pockets be reliably targeted in vivo without off‑target effects?

assets_to_track: Selective inhibitors designed against ABL inactive state two
